Skip to contents
jstats 0.9.98
  • Reference

Internal helper: validate named arguments captured via ...

dot-jst_check_args.Rd

Catches mis-named argument aliases that users sometimes type instead of the correct name and errors with a "Did you mean" suggestion. Also catches any other named argument in ... that isn't on the aliases list and errors with a plain unused-argument message. Used by functions that accept ... as a safety net (not for substantive variable-passing).

Usage

.jst_check_args(dots, aliases, fn_name)

Arguments

dots

A list of arguments captured via list(...).

aliases

Named character vector. Names are the incorrect argument names that users might type; values are the correct argument names to suggest in the error message.

fn_name

Character. The calling function's name, used in the error message.

Value

invisible(NULL). Called for its side effect of throwing an error when an invalid argument name is found.

On this page

Developed by Jeff Ackerman.
